tool: fulcrum insnattr — CLOSED per-region gz - libdeflate differential (M1 silesia T1)
note_head: HEAD of reimplement-isa-l = 909fe598; the only diff 3d59..909fe598 is the multi-symbol litlen-LUT skip in the MARKER path (lut_huffman.rs); LitLenTable::build (libdeflate_entry.rs) is BYTE-IDENTICAL at both commits, so the BUILD differential below holds at HEAD.
method: kpc whole-program retired TOTAL (load-immune, median N=9, A/A spread 0.11%) x /usr/bin/sample per-symbol distribution. Gate-0 ALL PASS both arms (sha==oracle, kpc stable, sample non-inert, decode-dominated, A/A-stable).
limit_gate5: per-symbol split is TIME-weighted sample distribution (retired-exact only under uniform per-symbol IPC; gz/ld whole-program IPC ratio 1.093). Per-symbol SHARE sampling noise observed ~12M across runs (LitLenTable::build 86.6M..98.3M). A build change below ~12M cannot be gated by insnattr; a dedicated build-microbench (kpc over N isolated build calls) is the PULLED prerequisite to gate a build lever.

STEP1_libdeflate_is_separate_not_inlined.fastloop_monolith: libdeflate_deflate_decompress_ex = 1342.3M (91.2% of ld) — fastloop+header+offset+loop-control, NOT splittable by symbol
STEP1_libdeflate_is_separate_not_inlined.harness_poll: poll = 49.2M (sample-harness loop; appears in BOTH arms, ~cancels)

STEP2_closed_per_region_differential.ld_detail: the monolith (includes ld's own header parse + offset + loop-control)
STEP2_closed_per_region_differential.verdict: DOMINANT but NOT splittable in instructions on M1 (monolith + time-weighting = the documented attribution wall). CROSS-CHECK FLAG (HYPOTHESIS, not asserted): gz clean_decode ALONE (1521.3M) EXCEEDS ld's ENTIRE decode (1472.6M); time-weighting bias (gz IPC higher) does NOT rescue parity, so the prior 'fastloop at per-symbol PARITY' claim is CONTRADICTED by whole-symbol attribution and must be RE-MEASURED, not assumed.
CONFIRMED clean surplus (both are isolated table-construction symbols). The HAVE's '~87M candidate' is CONFIRMED (+81.5M, 3.12x). Single largest CLEANLY-ATTRIBUTABLE per-region surplus.

gz CRC32Calculator::update is ~1.9x ld's crc32_arm_pmullx12_crc_eor3 in INSTRUCTIONS (contradicts the HAVE 'gz crc AHEAD' which was active-TIME). Candidate clean lever: adopt eor3-pmull. Separate from build.

gz _platform_memmove >> ld. Likely ParallelSM chunk-output assembly (apply_window/replace_markers), i.e. architecture overhead, NOT the inner match copy. (Inner match_copy differential is the active-time critpath artifact's 2.42x.)
ParallelSM T1 pipeline overhead (thread-local, memset for chunk buffers, block_finder) that ld's bare single-shot lacks. poll (~50M) cancels. Architecture, out of inner-loop scope.

conservation_check: 239.9+81.5+26.4+31.8+30.2 = 409.8M ~= measured surplus 409.9M. Both sides conservation-closed to their kpc retired totals.

structural_reasons_gz_heavier (read of L: STRIDED scalar stores per symbol (entries[idx]=e; idx+=stride) — libdeflate explicitly AVOIDS this via incremental table-DOUBLING (memcpy first-half->second-half, vectorizable). This is the dominant cost driver.

PER-BLOCK Vec alloc + zero-init (vec![LitLenEntry(0); N]) — libdeflate reuses a fixed buffer in the decompressor struct and never zero-inits (a complete code fills every entry). gz's zero-init is LOAD-BEARING for incomplete-litlen-code correctness, so it is NOT safely removable.

LENGTH PRE-EXPANSION (gz bakes extra-bits into separate main-table entries for length codes) — libdeflate defers extra-bits to decode time. This is EXTRA build work gz does deliberately to speed the fastloop.
why_not: The dominant driver (strided stores -> table-doubling) is ENTANGLED with pre-expansion: doubling fills one canonical entry per codeword in lex order, which pre-expansion's sub-stride multi-entry fills break. Adopting doubling cleanly means dropping pre-expansion (a fastloop RE-TRADE, not a free build win) OR a doubling-then-overwrite-length-slots rewrite.
Buffer-reuse + zero-init removal save only ~9-15M (memset/bzero) and zero-init is unsafe to remove (incomplete codes).
Any sub-rewrite saving < ~12M is BELOW the M1 insnattr per-symbol sampling noise floor and cannot pass Gate-1 (Δ<spread = TIE).
STEP3_build_attack_assessment.pulled_next_tool: fulcrum buildbench — isolate LitLenTable::build vs libdeflate build_decode_table on the REAL per-block silesia code-length sets, kpc over N iterations -> tight-CI build-only retired count. This is the prerequisite to GATE the doubling/buffer-reuse rewrite below the 12M noise.

PLAIN_VERDICT: The +21%/+27.8% M1-T1 gz-libdeflate instruction gap is BOTH: (1) a DOMINANT fastloop+header surplus (+239.9M, 58.5% of surplus) that is NOT splittable in instructions on M1 and whose 'per-symbol parity' is CONTRADICTED by whole-symbol attribution (gz clean_decode alone > ld whole decode) — RE-MEASURE, do not assume parity; and (2) a CONFIRMED table-BUILD surplus (+81.5M, 3.12x, 19.9% of surplus) — the largest cleanly-attributable lever, but its reduction requires a structural table-doubling/buffer-reuse rewrite entangled with the pre-expansion fastloop trade (NOT a clean local byte-exact change), and is gateable only with a dedicated build-microbench. Secondary clean candidates: crc eor3-pmull (+26M), copy/memmove (+32M, mostly ParallelSM architecture).
